Committee.CommitteeName: Substance Abuse Prevention and Treatment Task Force

RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 407-C, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
Representative Becky Laubinger, a Republican, proudly represents St. Francois County (District 117) in the Missouri House of Representatives. She was elected to her first two-year term in November 2024.
A resident of St. Francois County since 2009, Rep. Laubinger has devoted her career to service and community engagement. She graduated from Missouri Baptist University in 1998 with a Bachelor of Arts degree in Religious Studies, which shaped her passion for faith-based outreach and family support. For over a decade, she has served as the executive director of Monarch Family Resource Center, a nonprofit organization dedicated to providing critical resources and support to families in need, including parenting education, crisis intervention, and youth mentorship.
Rep. Laubinger is deeply committed to her faith and community. She is an active member of the First Baptist Church of Desloge, where her husband, Bradford, serves as pastor. In addition, she contributes her leadership skills as a member of the Nominating Committee for the Mineral Area Baptist Association, served on the Resolutions Committee for the Missouri Baptist Convention, was a community representative for Children’s Division mandated meetings, coached through Upward Sports, and participates in the Desloge, Bonne Terre, and Park Hills-Leadington Chambers of Commerce. Rep. Laubinger currently serves as a representative on the Central R-III Parents as Teachers board.
Rep. Laubinger and her husband, Bradford, are proud parents to four children and have one daughter-in-law. In her free time, she enjoys spending time with her family, organizing community events, and participating in local outreach programs that reflect her commitment to serving others.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Modifies provisions relating to tax credits
Creates provisions relating to regulation of online content involving minors
Prohibits the use of fetal organs or tissue resulting from abortions for medical, scientific, research, experimental, or therapeutic purposes or any other use
Modifies provisions relating to child protection
Modifies provisions of the diaper bank tax credit
Modifies provisions governing school employee training requirements

CoSponsoredBill.LongTitle: Provides protections against discrimination and antisemitism in public schools and public postsecondary educational institutions
Modifies provisions relating to an income tax credit for contributions to pregnancy resource center
Designates the last week in April each year as "Infertility Awareness Week"
